title: Theme system — CSS-based, pluggable
description: Build the theme infrastructure: a CSS-based theme loader, a consistent set of CSS custom properties (design tokens) that all themes must implement, and the integration with the generator. Themes are plain CSS files in src/themes/ — no build step, no preprocessor.

features: Define the standard CSS custom property contract that every theme must implement (document in src/themes/README.md): --bg-color, --bg-secondary, --text-primary, --text-secondary, --text-muted, --accent-color, --accent-hover, --link-bg, --link-bg-hover, --link-border, --link-radius, --link-padding, --font-body, --font-mono, --avatar-radius, --page-max-width, --footer-opacity
Implement src/themes/loader.js: loadTheme(themeName) — resolves theme name to CSS file path, reads and returns CSS string, throws descriptive error if theme not found
Implement src/themes/loader.js: listThemes() — returns array of available theme names (reads src/themes/*.css filenames)
The generator (src/generator.js) calls loadTheme(config.theme) and inlines the CSS into the <style> block
Theme CSS files must define all custom properties on :root and use them throughout — no hardcoded colors outside :root
Create src/themes/README.md documenting the custom property contract and how to create a new theme

notes:
[GIA REJECTED] Hook(s) failed: 001-adr-compliance.py. Alignment score 0.8333 below threshold 1.0000
Theme loader uses import.meta.url + fileURLToPath to resolve src/themes/ relative to the module — works with ES modules. listThemes() uses fs.readdirSync filtering for .css extension.
